description:
You are acting as the build-engineer for a small C I system.  
    Three build logs are already present under /home/user/builds/logs/ :

        /home/user/builds/logs/build_2024-06-01.log
        /home/user/builds/logs/build_2024-06-02.log
        /home/user/builds/logs/build_2024-06-03.log

    Your job is to inspect those log files and produce two machine-readable reports
    in /home/user/builds/reports/ :

        1) /home/user/builds/reports/daily_summary.csv  
        2) /home/user/builds/reports/latest_error.txt  

    Create /home/user/builds/reports/ if it does not already exist and make sure it is writable by the current user.

    IMPORTANT – exact output formats the automated grader will check:

    1. daily_summary.csv  
       • CSV, comma-separated, **no extra spaces**, UTF-8, Unix new-lines (LF).  
       • First line is the header exactly:  
         filename,total_lines,info_count,warn_count,error_count  
       • One subsequent line per *.log file, listed in **ascending
         alphabetical order of the file name**.  
       • Example of correct structure (numbers are ONLY an illustration, do not copy them):  
         filename,total_lines,info_count,warn_count,error_count  
         build_2024-01-01.log,15,10,3,2  
       • Do NOT append a trailing comma or any blank lines.

    2. latest_error.txt  
       • Plain text, UTF-8, Unix new-lines (LF).  
       • Exactly three lines, in this order:  
           Line 1 – the timestamp of the most recent ERROR entry in the logs, **without brackets** (format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS).  
           Line 2 – the file name in which that ERROR appears (e.g. build_2024-06-03.log).  
           Line 3 – the full ERROR message text after the log-level marker, without the level label, without enclosing brackets.  
       • No extra whitespace before or after any line.

    Definition of log-levels you must count:  
        – A line is INFO if it contains “] INFO ” after the timestamp.  
        – A line is WARN if it contains “] WARN ” after the timestamp.  
        – A line is ERROR if it contains “] ERROR ” after the timestamp.  
    Every physical line is counted toward total_lines, regardless of content.

    What you need to do (high-level steps, do **NOT** output the commands here):
        • Create the /home/user/builds/reports/ directory if necessary.  
        • Read every *.log file in /home/user/builds/logs/.  
        • Count total, INFO, WARN and ERROR lines for each file.  
        • Identify the chronologically latest ERROR over all logs.  
        • Generate daily_summary.csv and latest_error.txt exactly as described.  
        • It is fine (even recommended) to print your own progress information to the terminal, but the two report files must strictly follow the formats above.

    Verification considerations:  
        • The grader will parse daily_summary.csv, check the header, row order and the five numeric columns.  
        • The grader will read latest_error.txt and compare all three lines to ground truth.  
        • The original log files must not be modified or deleted.

    Hints (not mandatory):  
        • GNU sort can help with alphabetical ordering of file names.  
        • date -d or GNU coreutils can help convert timestamps if you need to compare them.  
        • Using awk/grep/sed is usually enough; no need for external libraries.

    Once you are done, make sure both report files exist at the exact paths and run “cat /home/user/builds/reports/daily_summary.csv” and
    “cat /home/user/builds/reports/latest_error.txt” so the results are visible in the console output.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing directory structure & files
    -->
    Directory: /home/user/builds/logs/

    File: /home/user/builds/logs/build_2024-06-01.log
    --------------------------------------------------
    [2024-06-01 08:00:01] INFO Build started
    [2024-06-01 08:00:05] WARN Deprecated API used
    [2024-06-01 08:00:10] INFO Compiling module A
    [2024-06-01 08:00:20] ERROR Failed to compile module A: missing header
    [2024-06-01 08:00:30] INFO Retrying
    [2024-06-01 08:00:40] INFO Compiled module A
    [2024-06-01 08:01:00] INFO Build finished
    --------------------------------------------------

    File: /home/user/builds/logs/build_2024-06-02.log
    --------------------------------------------------
    [2024-06-02 08:00:01] INFO Build started
    [2024-06-02 08:00:05] INFO Loading cache
    [2024-06-02 08:00:06] WARN Cache miss for module B
    [2024-06-02 08:00:08] INFO Compiling module B
    [2024-06-02 08:00:18] INFO Compiled module B
    [2024-06-02 08:00:20] INFO Build finished
    --------------------------------------------------

    File: /home/user/builds/logs/build_2024-06-03.log
    --------------------------------------------------
    [2024-06-03 08:00:01] INFO Build started
    [2024-06-03 08:00:03] INFO Loading cache
    [2024-06-03 08:00:05] INFO Compiling module C
    [2024-06-03 08:00:15] ERROR Failed to compile module C: syntax error
    [2024-06-03 08:00:25] ERROR Failed to compile module C: unresolved reference
    [2024-06-03 08:00:40] WARN Low disk space
    [2024-06-03 08:01:00] INFO Aborting build
    --------------------------------------------------


    Ground-truth counts for daily_summary.csv
    (filename,total_lines,info_count,warn_count,error_count):

        build_2024-06-01.log,7,5,1,1
        build_2024-06-02.log,6,5,1,0
        build_2024-06-03.log,7,4,1,2

    Therefore the correct daily_summary.csv MUST contain exactly:

        filename,total_lines,info_count,warn_count,error_count
        build_2024-06-01.log,7,5,1,1
        build_2024-06-02.log,6,5,1,0
        build_2024-06-03.log,7,4,1,2

    Latest ERROR details (for latest_error.txt):

        Timestamp : 2024-06-03 08:00:25
        File      : build_2024-06-03.log
        Message   : Failed to compile module C: unresolved reference

    So latest_error.txt must be exactly:

        Line 1: 2024-06-03 08:00:25
        Line 2: build_2024-06-03.log
        Line 3: Failed to compile module C: unresolved reference

    When the grader finishes, it will open:
      /home/user/builds/reports/daily_summary.csv  (must match exactly)
      /home/user/builds/reports/latest_error.txt   (must match exactly)

    No other file or extra content is needed for verification.
